examples.title: Ordering Mixed Numbers
examples.content:
Order from smallest to largest: $-1.5, 0, 2, -3, 0.5, \sqrt{2}$.

**Solution**: Place on number line and read left to right:
$$-3 < -1.5 < 0 < 0.5 < \sqrt{2} < 2$$

Note: $\sqrt{2} \approx 1.414$, which falls between 0.5 and 2.

examples.title: Applying Inequality Properties
examples.content:
Given $3 < 7$, what happens when you multiply both sides by $-2$?

**Solution**: When multiplying by a negative number, REVERSE the inequality sign:
$$3 < 7$$
$$3 \times (-2) \text{ ? } 7 \times (-2)$$
$$-6 > -14$$

Verify: Is $-6 > -14$? Yes! $-6$ is to the right of $-14$ on the number line.
